description_long: This is a World War II-era military airplane with a distinctive camouflage paint scheme in green, brown, and light gray, featuring black crosses and insignia on the wings and fuselage. The aircraft has a low-wing monoplane configuration with straight wings and visible dihedral angles, and is equipped with two large propellers mounted on the nose. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is positioned forward on the fuselage and is enclosed with a clear canopy, likely accommodating two crew members in tandem. The landing gear is of the taildragger type, with two main wheels under the wings and a smaller tail wheel.
description: A camouflaged WWII military airplane with low straight wings, twin propellers, and a conventional tail, featuring black crosses and an enclosed forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the camouflage pattern, propeller, and tail details, with visible insignia and cockpit canopy.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the twin propellers, wing dihedral, and landing gear arrangement.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same camouflage, insignia, and structural features as view 0.
description_view_3: Head-on view emphasizing the propeller, cockpit canopy, and the symmetry of the wings and landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing monoplane with straight wings and moderate dihedral angle, no winglets or swept features.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the nose with two-bladed propellers.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed gear under the wings and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a clear canopy, likely tandem seating.
